name: run-nx-generator
description: Run Nx generators with prioritization for workspace-plugin generators. Use this when generating code, scaffolding new features, or automating repetitive tasks in the monorepo.
capabilities: Prioritizes workspace-plugin generators for custom internal tooling
Lists available Nx generators with schema information
Provides command syntax for running generators
Guides dry-run preview before applying changes
Verifies generated code with tests and formatting
author: nrwl
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill providing guidance on running Nx generators. All 29 static findings are false positives: MD5 hashes are content identifiers in metadata, backticks are markdown code formatting, URLs are source references, and the skill contains no executable code, network requests, or external command execution.
valueStatement: Generating code in monorepos requires navigating complex project structures and selecting the right generator. This skill prioritizes workspace-plugin generators for custom solutions and guides you through running generators with proper verification steps.
userTitle: Run Nx generators for monorepo code generation
limitations: Does not execute generators directly, requires user to run nx commands
Does not create or modify Nx workspace configuration
Does not install or update Nx packages
Does not manage generator options or presets

useCases.title: Generate code in monorepos
useCases.description: Scaffold new features, libraries, and components across multiple projects in an Nx workspace

useCases.title: Update workspace plugins
useCases.description: Run custom workspace-plugin generators to maintain and update internal tooling and configurations

useCases.title: Automate version migrations
useCases.description: Execute migration generators for version updates and configuration changes across projects

promptTemplates.title: List generators
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the available Nx generators in this workspace

promptTemplates.title: Generator schema
promptTemplates.prompt: What options are available for the @nx/workspace-plugin:create-nodes-plugin generator

promptTemplates.title: Preview changes
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a dry-run of the @nx/react:component generator for my new component

promptTemplates.title: Run generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a new Node.js library called utils in the shared directory

bestPractices: Check workspace-plugin generators first for custom solutions
Use --dry-run flag to preview changes before applying
Format and test generated code immediately after generation
antiPatterns: Using core generators when workspace-plugin generators have custom solutions
Skipping the dry-run preview before running generators
Forgetting to run tests after generating new code

faq.question: What Nx versions are supported?
faq.answer: Works with Nx 16+ workspaces. Check your nx.json for version compatibility.

faq.question: What are the generator limits?
faq.answer: Generator availability depends on installed plugins. Core Nx and workspace plugins are prioritized.

faq.question: Can I integrate with CI pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. Use nx generate in scripts and nx affected -t test,lint,build for CI validation.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: This skill only reads generator documentation. No data is stored or transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why is my generator not found?
faq.answer: Ensure the plugin is installed and listed in nx.json. Use mcp__nx-mcp__nx_generators to list available generators.

faq.question: How does this compare to nx CLI?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance and best practices. The actual nx CLI commands must be run manually in the terminal.
